event: Study for the Temptation of Saint Anthony
symbols: semi-nude female figure — the demonic temptation of lust that assailed Anthony in the desert
dark shadowy figure behind — the devil lurking behind the alluring facade of temptation
red garments — passion, sensuality, and the fires of temptation
description: A pastel or chalk sketch on paper showing a semi-nude female figure in profile, facing left. She wears a red hat or headdress and a red skirt or drapery around her lower body, with dark gloves or sleeves on her arms. Her torso is bare. Behind her, a shadowy dark figure is faintly visible. The inscription at the top reads 'La Tentation de St. Antoine.' The drawing is loose and impressionistic, with areas of red, green, and black pastel against the cream paper. A faint sketched figure appears at the lower left.
theological_description: The Temptation of Saint Anthony is one of the most enduring subjects in Christian art, drawn from the account of Anthony the Great (c. 251-356), the father of Christian monasticism, who withdrew into the Egyptian desert where he was assailed by demonic temptations. Athanasius's Life of Anthony records that the devil appeared in many forms — including that of a seductive woman — to lure the saint from his ascetic commitment. This struggle embodies the universal Christian battle against the flesh described by Paul: 'The flesh desires what is contrary to the Spirit, and the Spirit what is contrary to the flesh' (Gal 5:17). Anthony's victory through prayer and resistance became the model for all subsequent monastic spirituality. James 4:7 promises, 'Resist the devil, and he will flee from you,' a truth Anthony proved through decades of desert combat. The shadowy figure behind the temptress suggests the true author of the deception — Satan himself disguised as an angel of light (2 Cor 11:14).

scripture_refs.reason: The flesh warring against the Spirit — the interior battle Anthony endured in the desert
Resist the devil and he will flee — the principle Anthony embodied through persistent resistance
Satan disguising himself as an angel of light — the deceptive beauty of the temptress hiding demonic intent
The devil prowls like a roaring lion seeking whom he may devour — the spiritual danger Anthony faced

_meta.title: Study for The Temptation of Saint Anthony
